title: tt windows config
description: Per-project tmux layout for `tt up`, read from <project>/.tt/windows.json. Absent => tt's built-in default (dev + claude). Configures FIXED windows only; the lazy pi-* worker pool is tt-owned and not configurable here. Every pane cmd is applied with a bare-shell guard: a cmd is (re)sent only when its pane is a bare shell, so `tt up` is idempotent and reboots self-heal. A run command (enter:true) resumes into a now-bare pane on a later `tt up`; a typed prefill (enter:false) fires only on fresh creation (never re-typed). Requires jq; without it tt falls back to the legacy dev+claude layout and ignores this file.

dev.description: Primary work window (tmux window 0). Default name 'dev'. Default panes: one bare shell.

claude.description: Orchestrator window — the attach/focus target on `tt up`. Default name 'claude'. Default panes: one pane running `claude --continue --allow-dangerously-skip-permissions || claude --allow-dangerously-skip-permissions`. To use a different coding agent, set claude.panes[0].cmd (e.g. "pi").

extra_windows.description: Additional fixed windows, created in order AFTER dev+claude and BEFORE the pi-* pool.

pane.description: One pane. {} or an omitted cmd => bare shell.

cmd.description: Command typed into the pane, sent only when the pane is a bare shell.

enter.description: Press Enter after cmd. false => pre-type only and never re-send on a later `tt up` (e.g. a tunnel cmd needing an ssh-agent key loaded first).

name.description: tmux window name. Defaults to the role key (dev / claude).

layout.description: tmux layout applied after splitting. Default: even-horizontal when >1 pane.

panes.description: Panes in split order (left-to-right / top-to-bottom). Omit to use the role default.

namedWindow.description: An extra window — name is required (no role key to default from).
